seoTitle: Embed Excel in Confluence: 2026 Live Spreadsheet Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to embed Excel in Confluence in 2026 by uploading a workbook or syncing OneDrive, so your team always sees a live, single source-of-truth sheet.
intro: Jumping to a separate tab to check a budget slows everyone down, and copy-pasted spreadsheets lose their formulas. This guide shows how to embed a live Microsoft Excel workbook directly inside a Confluence page in 2026.

steps.heading: Open the file uploader
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type `/excel` or `/file` to deploy the media and Office upload block.

steps.heading: Upload a local file
steps.body: Click Upload to browse your system and drag your Excel workbook directly into the container.

steps.heading: Or connect a cloud file
steps.body: Paste a secure Microsoft 365 or OneDrive sharing URL to generate an interactive live card instead.

steps.heading: Adjust the viewer
steps.body: Click the embedded frame to set the viewport height, toggle gridlines, and select specific sheet tabs.

steps.heading: Publish the page
steps.body: Review your layout and click Publish to save your settings and go live.

faq.question: Will the embedded Excel file update when I edit the original?
faq.answer: Yes, when connected through OneDrive or SharePoint, the embedded view updates automatically when the cloud file changes.

faq.question: Can I embed just one sheet from a multi-tab workbook?
faq.answer: Yes, the viewer settings let you select a default sheet tab to display.

faq.question: Do I need to upload a file, or can I link to an existing one?
faq.answer: You can do either — upload a local .xlsx file directly, or paste a OneDrive or SharePoint link to render an existing cloud file.

faq.question: Will embedding a large spreadsheet slow down my page?
faq.answer: The 2026 database architecture handles external spreadsheet streams so grids render smoothly without slowing text loading.
